action.reasoning: Observation: Iter 7 training ran for 2.8h on 4 GPUs (DataParallel) but was killed with exit code 137 (likely OOM). Training curves show loss decreasing but NaN at step 890. No checkpoint saved. Analysis: DataParallel with SegFormer's internal loss computation caused memory issues and process kill. After 7 iterations we still have no baseline mIoU. Decision: Switch to DDP (torchrun) with manual loss computation, proper DistributedSampler, and validation on rank 0 only. This is a proven approach for segmentation with HF models. Use batch_size=4 per GPU (total 16). Train 2 epochs to get baseline mIoU and measure iteration time. Update predict.py to resize images to short side 512, infer, and resize masks back to original size. Submit eval after training.
